probes.name: add_research_topic
probes.prompt: Hey, can you add a research topic for me? I want to monitor what's new with the RP2040 microcontroller ecosystem — new dev boards, firmware updates, community projects. Set it up as a weekly check.
probes.criteria:
The agent acts as the /research command trigger. A good response confirms it has created a new research topic entry for RP2040 ecosystem developments, specifies the monitoring frequency (weekly), and lists the kinds of sources or search terms it will track (dev boards, firmware updates, community projects). It should not hallucinate results — just confirm setup and explain what will be monitored.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message 12 ('where are things like car invoices, or taxes?') and message 19 ('You have tool for that and subagent') — the user delegates tracking/search tasks conversationally.

probes.name: list_monitored_websites
probes.prompt: What websites are you currently monitoring for me? And what search queries do you have set up? Just list them all.
probes.criteria:
The agent acts as the /research command trigger managing research topics and monitored websites. A good response lists all currently configured monitored websites and active periodic search queries the user has set up, or honestly states that none exist yet if the system is empty. It should reference actual data from research_manager.py / website_monitor.py rather than fabricating entries.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

probes.name: add_polish_website_monitor
probes.prompt: Dodaj mi proszę stronę do monitorowania — sprawdzaj codziennie czy pojawiły się nowe oferty mieszkań na wynajem w Warszawie na otodom.pl, szukaj dwupokojowych do 3500 zł.
probes.criteria:
The agent acts as the /research command trigger for managing monitored websites. A good response confirms adding otodom.pl as a monitored website with a daily check frequency, specifies the search parameters (Warsaw, 2-room apartments up to 3500 PLN), and explains how the user will be notified of new listings. It should handle Polish-language input naturally and respond in Polish.

probes.name: remove_search_query
probes.prompt: I don't need the search for mechanical keyboard switches anymore, can you remove that query? Also while you're at it, what's the status on the last run of the NVIDIA job listings monitor?
probes.criteria:
The agent acts as the /research command trigger managing periodic search queries and website monitors. A good response (a) confirms removal of the specified search query for mechanical keyboard switches, and (b) reports the status/timestamp of the last run of the NVIDIA job listings monitor, or states if no such monitor exists. It handles both a deletion and a status check in one exchange.

probes.name: update_monitor_frequency
probes.prompt: Zmień częstotliwość sprawdzania tego monitora na temat teorii grafów — sprawdzaj co dwa dni zamiast codziennie, bo za dużo alertów dostaję. I dodaj też nowe słowa kluczowe: 'graph neural networks' i 'GNN benchmark'.
probes.criteria:
The agent acts as the /research command trigger managing research topics and their parameters. A good response (a) confirms updating the existing graph theory topic's monitoring frequency from daily to every two days, and (b) confirms adding the new keywords 'graph neural networks' and 'GNN benchmark' to the search terms. It should respond clearly in Polish matching the user's language and confirm each change separately.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
